summary: Linked Yoshi neutral special by shrinking `ftYs_SpecialN.c` to the 0x8012CC6C-0x8012DF18 range, moving the later Yoshi special routines into a new `ftYs_SpecialHi.c`, and converting `ftYs_SpecialN.c` from NonMatching to Matching. The cleanup also renamed several Yoshi egg/accessor helpers, added typed attribute access in place of raw offset reads, and gave Fighter::x1A60 the semantic name `target_item_gobj`. decomp.dev reported +1232 matched bytes, +4780 linked bytes, and 47 new matches; its 42 broken matches appear largely to be bookkeeping from moving functions out of the old `ftYs_SpecialN` unit and from relocating shield/accessor helpers.
what_changed: `configure.py` now marks `melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/ftYs_SpecialN.c` as `Object(Matching, ...)` and adds `ftYs_SpecialHi.c` as a separate `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.

`config/GALE01/splits.txt` was rebalanced: `ftYs_Guard.c` now ends at 0x8012CC6C, `ftYs_SpecialN.c` covers 0x8012CC6C-0x8012DF18 with only a small sdata2 range, and the new `ftYs_SpecialHi.c` covers 0x8012DF18-0x80132A0C.

Several functions previously named as `ftYs_Shield_8012CC6C` through `ftYs_Shield_8012CD00` were moved/renamed under `ftYs_SpecialN_*`, including `GetBoneIndex`, `SetupItemVel`, `GetFacingDir`, and `GetDatAttr18/1C/20/24`.

Several raw-offset ext-attribute helpers were renamed from address-style names to `ftYs_SpecialN_GetExtAttr28`, `GetExtAttr2C`, `GetExtAttr34`, and `GetExtAttr38`, while still preserving some address-style names where semantics were not clear.
`ftYs_SpecialN.c` replaced multiple `M2C_FIELD(gFtDataList[0xE]->ext_attr, ...)` accesses with typed `ftYoshiAttributes* ext_attr = gFtDataList[FTKIND_YOSHI]->ext_attr` reads.

`src/melee/ft/chara/ftYoshi/types.h` refined Yoshi attributes around offsets 0x28-0x4C, changing several fields to floats/ints, grouping 0x3C/0x40 as `Vec2 x3C`, and adding a smaller `struct ftYs_DatAttrs` for data attributes with fields at 0x10, 0x18, 0x1C, 0x20, and 0x24.
`src/melee/ft/types.h` renamed `Fighter::x1A60` to `target_item_gobj`; users in Yoshi, Kirby, fighter core, and helper inlines were updated accordingly.
`ftCo_YoshiEgg.c` now calls the renamed Yoshi SpecialN accessors for egg velocity, facing direction, dat attributes, grab timer behavior, and ext attributes.
`itCommonItems.h` renamed fields in `it_27CF_DatAttrs`, notably `float1` to `lifetime` and `x28` to `kind` with type `ItemKind`.
`ftCo_800BBC88` was promoted from a local static declaration in `ftCo_YoshiEgg.c` to a public declaration in `ftCo_YoshiEgg.h`.

The new `ftYs_SpecialHi.c` holds Yoshi special high/side/low routines that had been in the SpecialN address span, including `ftYs_SpecialHi_Anim`, `ftYs_SpecialAirHi_Anim`, `ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF18`, and many related state handlers.
smart_moves: The PR solved linking by respecting the actual object boundary: it did not just force more code into `ftYs_SpecialN.c`, but split the later address range into `ftYs_SpecialHi.c` and updated splits/symbols/config together.
Renaming shield-prefixed helpers to `ftYs_SpecialN_*` made cross-file call sites in Yoshi Egg read according to gameplay ownership rather than accidental previous file placement.

Keeping uncertain functions as address-style names while renaming only obvious getters avoided overclaiming semantics; examples include preserving names like `ftYs_SpecialN_8012CD40`, `ftYs_SpecialN_8012CD88`, and `ftYs_SpecialN_8012CDD4`.
Typed ext-attribute access via `FTKIND_YOSHI` and `ftYoshiAttributes` reduced reliance on magic fighter kind `0xE` and raw `M2C_FIELD` offsets.
Giving `Fighter::x1A60` the name `target_item_gobj` helped clarify shared Yoshi/Kirby capture-item logic without changing struct layout.

The bot metrics show net progress despite many reported broken matches, which is consistent with a careful unit relocation: functions moved from old units to the new `ftYs_SpecialHi` unit while overall linked/matched bytes increased.
decomp_lessons: When a large character file contains routines for multiple specials, check `splits.txt` and symbol address ranges before trying to make the whole file match; a split object can turn an apparent nonmatch into a linkable unit.
Function names should follow the owning move/system when call sites reveal semantics. The 0x8012CC6C-0x8012CD00 helpers were better classified as `SpecialN` egg helpers than as shield helpers.
Offset-suffixed getter names like `GetDatAttr24` and `GetExtAttr38` are useful intermediate names when the data source is known but the gameplay meaning is not yet known.

Struct field refinement can be driven by access patterns: repeated float reads at ext_attr offsets 0x28, 0x2C, 0x30, 0x34 and an integer at 0x38 justified changing the Yoshi attribute layout away from generic `s32`/`f32` placeholders.
Renaming a common fighter field requires sweeping unrelated users, such as Kirby capture logic and fighter core grab processing, because shared capture/item mechanics cross character boundaries.
decomp.dev's per-unit 'broken matches' can be misleading after file moves; compare net matched/linked bytes and inspect whether broken symbols reappear in the new unit.
naming_conventions: `ftYs_SpecialN_GetBoneIndex` for a helper returning a `Fighter_Part` bone index.
`ftYs_SpecialN_SetupItemVel` for a helper filling a `Vec3*` output velocity.
`ftYs_SpecialN_GetDatAttrXX` for getters reading per-fighter dat attributes at hex offsets such as 0x18, 0x1C, 0x20, and 0x24.
`ftYs_SpecialN_GetExtAttrXX` for getters reading Yoshi global/ext attributes at hex offsets such as 0x28, 0x2C, 0x34, and 0x38.
`target_item_gobj` is the semantic replacement for `Fighter::x1A60` when the field stores the item object currently targeted/captured by fighter logic.
`it_27CF_DatAttrs::lifetime` and `it_27CF_DatAttrs::kind` are preferable to generic `float1` and `x28` when the item spawn attributes reveal meaning.
`ftYs_DatAttrs` was introduced as a Yoshi-specific dat-attribute struct separate from the broader `ftYoshiAttributes` ext-attribute layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Object-boundary tactic: move the 0x8012DF18-0x80132A0C portion into `ftYs_SpecialHi.c`, leaving `ftYs_SpecialN.c` as a smaller matching object.
Symbol-table tactic: rename symbols at fixed addresses without changing addresses or sizes, e.g. 0x8012CC6C became `ftYs_SpecialN_GetBoneIndex` and 0x8012CC94 became `ftYs_SpecialN_SetupItemVel`.

Data-section tactic: adjust rodata/sdata/sdata2 ownership in `splits.txt`; Yoshi init gained rodata and expanded sdata/sdata2, SpecialN's data footprint shrank, and SpecialHi received the later sdata2 range.
Typed-access tactic: replace `M2C_FIELD(..., offset)` with struct fields on `ftYoshiAttributes` once field types and offsets are stable enough.

Callback/motion matching tactic: the new SpecialN implementation uses small static inlines such as setup/check/change helpers in the diff, suggesting local inline extraction was used to preserve compiler output while improving readability.
No-inline helper tactic is present elsewhere in the diff via `gmCamera_801A25C8_no_inline(void)`, but the PR context does not establish that it was central to matching Yoshi SpecialN.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report: matched code 38.93% (+0.03%, +1232 bytes) and linked code 25.71% (+0.12%, +4780 bytes).
decomp.dev listed 47 new matches, including `ftYs_SpecialN2_1_Anim`, `ftYs_SpecialAirN2_1_Anim`, and many functions now under `ftYs_SpecialHi`.

decomp.dev also listed 42 broken matches, mostly in the old `ftYs_SpecialN` unit and two former `ftYs_Guard` accessors, consistent with file splitting and symbol relocation rather than necessarily a net regression.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `ftYs_SpecialN_GetDatAttr18/1C/20/24` or explain their gameplay meanings.
Find later PRs that make `ftYs_SpecialHi.c` matching after this split.
Search for subsequent uses or renames of `target_item_gobj` to validate the Fighter::x1A60 semantic name.
Check whether `ftYs_DatAttrs` was expanded or merged with `ftYoshiAttributes` in later Yoshi cleanup.
Search for later cleanup of remaining address-style `ftYs_SpecialN_8012CD40`, `ftYs_SpecialN_8012CD88`, `ftYs_SpecialN_8012CDB4`, and `ftYs_SpecialN_8012CDD4`.
